input.location: An abandoned textile mill on the outskirts of a mid-size Northeastern US city, now partially converted to artist studios
input.scenes: Establishing shots of the exterior; a walk-through with a former mill worker (now 78) who worked there for 30 years; an interview with an artist who rents studio space in the converted section
input.film_context: Feature documentary about post-industrial communities and the gap between decline and reinvention
input.crew_size: 4 — director, two camera operators, sound recordist
input.equipment: No large lighting rigs; natural light preferred; one small LED panel for interview fill
input.time_of_day: Morning light preferred for exterior establishing shots

input.location: A public park with a large pond, used for a short interview sequence
input.scenes: One sit-down interview on a park bench

input.location: The interior of a decommissioned Berlin U-Bahn station (ghost station) that was sealed during the Cold War and has recently been opened for limited public tours
input.scenes: Walk-through with a Cold War historian; establishing shots of the station's preserved Cold War-era condition; a sit-down interview on the platform; atmospheric shots for the film's opening sequence
input.film_context: Documentary about the invisible traces of the Berlin Wall in the city's infrastructure — focusing on places where division is still physically present but hidden from daily life
input.equipment: Portable LED lighting essential — no natural light underground. Tripod and gimbal. No drones (underground).
input.sensitivities: Site is managed by Berliner Verkehrsbetriebe (BVG) and Berlin Unterwelten e.V.; access requires formal application. The station may have heritage protection status under Denkmalschutz. Some areas may be structurally restricted.
